query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed the research on KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell activity. Here's the summary:

## Key Findings

**Most recent data (July 2026)** — Foreign investors just flipped to buying:
- **July 9, 2026**: Net **bought +134.8 billion won** (KOSPI closed 7,291.91, +0.62%)
- **July 8, 2026**: Net **bought +343.6 billion won**
- These snapped a **13-session selling streak** (June 19–July 7)

**June 2026 was highly volatile:**
- Mid-June buying rally: +2.204 tn won (June 12), +1.077 tn (June 15), +1.535 tn (June 16)
- KOSPI hit an **all-time high of 9,114.55 on June 22**, then fell ~500 points toward 7,200

**The record "Sell Korea" wave (cumulative):**
- **H1 2026: record 148.32 trillion won** net selling (~$95–96.7 bn)
- **YTD through July 3: 157.3 trillion won** (~$102.5 bn)
- May–June alone: 92.89 tn won as the won weakened
- ~90% concentrated in Samsung Electronics & SK Hynix

**Notable paradox:** Despite record selling, KOSPI rose 91.9% YTD and foreign ownership share actually *rose* to 40.47% (from 36.65% in January) due to price appreciation. Retail investors absorbed the outflow, net buying 104.8 tn won YTD.

**Data caveat:** Sources report primarily daily flows and cumulative H1/YTD totals rather than clean weekly sums — the daily figures above best characterize the recent weekly trend. Most recent data point is July 9, 2026.

key_facts: July 9, 2026: Foreign investors net BOUGHT +134.8 billion won (~0.13 trillion won) of KOSPI shares; index closed 7,291.91 (+0.62%).
July 8, 2026: Foreign investors net BOUGHT +343.6 billion won (~0.34 trillion won), starting a reversal of the selling streak.
Foreigners were net sellers for 13 consecutive trading sessions from June 19 to July 7, 2026, before the July 8-9 buying.
First-half 2026 (Jan-Jun) foreign net selling was a record 148.32 trillion won (~$95-96.7 billion) on the KOSPI.
Through July 3, 2026, cumulative YTD foreign net selling reached 157.3 trillion won (~$102.5 billion).
May-June 2026 alone accounted for 92.89 trillion won of net selling as the won weakened against the dollar.
Mid-June rally: foreign net buys of +2.204 trillion won (June 12), +1.077 trillion won (June 15), +1.535 trillion won (June 16).
KOSPI hit an all-time high of 9,114.55 on June 22, 2026; first close above 9,000 on June 18, then fell ~500 points toward 7,200 by early July.
Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix accounted for ~90% of first-half foreign selling; Samsung foreign ownership hit a 16-year low.
Despite heavy selling, foreign ownership share of KOSPI rose to 40.47% (July 3) from 36.65% (Jan 2) due to price appreciation; KOSPI up 91.9% YTD through July 3.
Domestic retail investors net bought 104.8 trillion won YTD, absorbing foreign outflows.
KB Securities warned up to ~260 trillion won of additional foreign selling is possible if foreign ownership normalizes toward 35%.
